magnetization.configuration.title: Magnetization Config
magnetization.configuration.physics.tool: Tunable physics constants for the Sable sub-level query and the entity force application path.
magnetization.configuration.physics.stre: Multiplier applied to every emitter's tier force value. 1.0 = default. Set globally up or down to tune feel without editing each emitter.
magnetization.configuration.physics.lenz: Eddy-current braking on a magnetic ship moving over conductive (copper/aluminium) blocks — it drags to a slow float/coast like a magnet in a copper pipe. 0 disables; 1.0 = default; higher = stronger brake.
magnetization.configuration.physics.enti: Scale factor for converting impulse units to vanilla entity delta-movement per tick. Default 1/20 = 0.05.
magnetization.configuration.physics.coni: Cosine of the half-angle of conical emitters (Repulsor Coil). Larger value = narrower cone. cos(45°) = 0.7071.
magnetization.configuration.physics.maxA: Per-ship acceleration cap (m/s²) applied after Sable's mass scaling. Stops a STRONG emitter from launching a 1-block test ship across the world. 50 m/s² ≈ 5 g. Set to 0 to disable.
magnetization.configuration.physics.ship: Per-tick linear-velocity damping applied to ships under magnetic pull. 0.02 = 2% speed lost per tick a magnet is acting, so constant-force pulls settle to a terminal velocity instead of accelerating without bound. Set to 0 to disable drag.

Per-tick angular-velocity damping applied to ships under magnetic pull. Without it, the torque from off-center sample forces could keep a ship spinning indefinitely. 0.05 = 5% spin lost per tick. Set to 0 to disable.

Grid size for integrating a field over a ship's bounding box. 1 = single closest-point sample (1.0.0 behaviour); 3 = 3×3×3 = 27 samples that produce realistic torque on larger ships. Quadratic cost in the count.
Multiplier on external magnetic force a ship feels with no ferromagnetic blocks aboard. 1.0 = full strength regardless of composition.
Susceptibility added per ferromagnetic block on a ship. 0.05 = 20 iron blocks doubles responsiveness over baseline. Stacks with the per-magnet value.
Susceptibility added per magnet emitter block on a ship. Magnets count as ferrous-plus — their pole does NOT change ship polarity, only the strength.
Upper cap on a ship's susceptibility multiplier. Prevents a 500-block ferromagnetic airship from accelerating at 25× the rate of a small test cube. Set high (e.g. 100) to effectively disable.
How often (in ticks) a ship's magnetic state is rescanned. 20 = 1 s. Lower = more responsive to block changes but more CPU. Inverter place/break invalidates instantly; this catches everything else.
magnetization.configuration.physics.excl: An emitter on a contraption ignores its WHOLE assembly, not just the part it sits on. Stops a magnet on your aircraft from yanking its own bearing/spring-mounted subgroups — which looks wrong and can freeze the physics solver. Leave on unless you want emitters to act on connected subgroups of their own craft.
magnetization.configuration.compat.toolt: Tunings that only take effect when the named mod is installed; otherwise inert.
magnetization.configuration.compat.alexs: How our Magnetized status effect coexists with Alex's Caves' Magnetizing effect. BOTH (default, parallel); OURS_ONLY (AC effect swapped for ours on application); THEIRS_ONLY (our effect swapped for AC's).
magnetization.configuration.compat.field: Give players the Magnetization Field Manual (Patchouli guide book) on their first login. Per-player flag persists across reconnects. Requires Patchouli to be installed.
magnetization.configuration.compat.allow: Whether redstone signal activates the addon's redstone-powered emitters. Default true. Set false to force players to feed FE/RF — useful on hardcore servers.
Whether FE/RF energy activates the addon's emitters. Default true. Any FE-providing mod (C&A, Mekanism, Thermal, IE, AE2) works. Set false to disable energy-driven emitters.
magnetization.configuration.compat.emitt: Internal FE buffer size per emitter. 50 000 ≈ 4 minutes of continuous operation at the default drain.
FE consumed per tick while an emitter is energy-driven. 10 FE/tick = 200 FE/s. Set 0 to make energy power free.
Max FE/tick the emitter accepts from external sources. Higher = refills the buffer faster from heavy cabling.
magnetization.configuration.compat.anoma: When true (default), the vanilla compass spins erratically inside the Magnetic Anomaly biome — same theming as our Field Compass. Set false to leave vanilla behaviour untouched (useful if other mods rely on stable compass readings).
When true (default), Nature's Compass (if installed) also scrambles inside the anomaly. Set false to keep biome-search functional even inside the flux.
When true (default), Explorer's Compass (if installed) also scrambles inside the anomaly. Set false to keep structure-search functional even inside the flux.
magnetization.configuration.guiLimits.to: Admin caps on what each emitter's in-game GUI can dial up to. Prevents griefer-tier loadouts on shared servers.
magnetization.configuration.guiLimits.el: Highest strength tier an Electromagnet's GUI can select.
Highest range (blocks) an Electromagnet's GUI can dial up to.
magnetization.configuration.guiLimits.an: Highest strength tier a Magnetic Anchor's GUI can select.
Highest range (blocks) a Magnetic Anchor's GUI can dial up to.
magnetization.configuration.guiLimits.re: Highest strength tier a Repulsor Coil's GUI can select.
Highest range (blocks) a Repulsor Coil's GUI can dial up to.
magnetization.configuration.guiLimits.tr: Highest strength tier a Tractor Beam's GUI can select.
Highest range (blocks) a Tractor Beam's GUI can dial up to.
magnetization.configuration.guiLimits.ex: Highest strength tier an Excavator's GUI can select. Strength controls pull force and tunneling speed per in-flight ship.
Highest column depth (blocks) an Excavator may rip ores from. EXTREME-tier reaches bedrock from surface with a 6× tier multiplier (~192 blocks).
Hard cap on cells the Excavator's cone scan considers per pass. Safety against config typos.
Maximum ferromagnetic blocks one Magnetic Excavator may pull simultaneously. Each pulled block is a Sable sub-level, so this also caps physics-simulation cost.
magnetization.configuration.content.tool: Soft-disable individual blocks or items. Existing placements stay on the map but become inert; disabled entries are hidden from the creative tab.
magnetization.configuration.content.disa: Magnetization block paths to disable (e.g. "repulsor_coil"). Disabled blocks emit no field, are hidden from the creative tab, and skip their right-click GUI.
Magnetization item paths to disable (e.g. "magnetic_grapple"). Disabled items are hidden from the creative tab and their special effects skip.
magnetization.configuration.content.armo: Whether worn metal armor makes its wearer react to magnetic fields. On (default): players and armored mobs are pulled/pushed by emitters, magnetic ores and other fields through their gear. Off: armor is magnetically inert. Turn off if you keep getting dragged into ore deposits or hazards by your own armor.
magnetization.configuration.content.bloc: How right-clicking our interactible blocks (Electromagnet, Anchor, Repulsor, Tractor Beam, Excavator, Permanent/Temporary Magnet) behaves while holding a block. On (default): right-click places the block; shift-right-click opens the GUI / flips polarity. Off (legacy): right-click interacts, shift-right-click places. Empty hand or a non-block item always interacts on right-click.
magnetization.configuration.content.magn: Master switch for the passive magnetite → maghemite inventory decay. Off by default — players found the slow rusting of hoarded magnetite more confusing than satisfying. Turn on to re-enable.
Ticks a stamped magnetite stack waits before converting to its maghemite equivalent. Default 168000 = 1 in-game week (7 days). Only consulted when magnetiteOxidationEnabled is true.
magnetization.configuration.items.toolti: Cooldowns, ranges, and other knobs for the Magnetic Grapple and Field Compass items.
magnetization.configuration.items.grappl: Game ticks between Magnetic Grapple right-clicks. 20 = 1 second.
Maximum scan range (blocks) the Grapple uses to find an attractive emitter.
magnetization.configuration.items.compas: Scan radius (blocks) the Field Compass uses to list active fields.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.too: Optional flavor passes layered on top of standard worldgen. The base magnetite ore vein always generates in every overworld biome regardless of these flags.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.ano: If true, the Magnetic Anomaly biome spawns naturally and its runtime effects activate inside it (compass spin, 1.5× emitter strength, chaos field). Off = biome resource still loads (so /locate biome + /magnetization tp work) but it won't spawn naturally and effects stay inert.

How rare the anomaly biome is when generating naturally. EXTREMELY_RARE (default) pins a single climate slot — you may go tens of thousands of blocks without seeing one. VERY_RARE / RARE widen the slot incrementally. COMMON is mostly for testing.
Multiplier on the anomaly's chaos-field impulses (ships, players, items). 1.0 = default, 0 disables the chaos field while leaving compass spin + emitter bonus intact, higher values amplify.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.pet: If true, the Petrified Forest biome registers a TerraBlender region so it spawns naturally (a cold/dry inland slot). Off = no natural generation; /locate biome and /magnetization tp petrified_forest still work.
How rare the petrified forest biome is when generating naturally. Default RARE — encounterable on most overworld runs but not in every biome row. See anomalyBiomeRarity for what each tier means.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.mag: If true, sparse patches of magnetic_gravel appear in vanilla overworld biomes at very low frequency (~1-in-24 chunks). Lets survival players who never visit an Anomaly biome still find a small supply through normal exploration. Off = magnetic_gravel is strictly anomaly-exclusive.
magnetization.configuration.lightning.to: Lightning-Induced Remnant Magnetism (LIRM). Real-world phenomenon — a lightning strike's transient field stamps polarity onto nearby ferromagnetic material.
magnetization.configuration.lightning.li: If true, every lightning bolt magnetizes one unstamped metal armor/tool piece on the struck entity and has a high chance of converting nearby log blocks to petrified wood.
magnetization.configuration.tools.toolti: Per-tool signature abilities for magnetized tools. The shared 'pull dropped items' behavior still runs regardless of these toggles.
magnetization.configuration.tools.swordY: Magnetized swords yank opposite-pole armored targets one step toward the attacker on hit, on top of the Magnetized effect.
magnetization.configuration.tools.pickax: Sneaking with a magnetized pickaxe rips nearby ferromagnetic ore blocks out of the world as items, periodically.
Block radius scanned around the player for ferromagnetic ores while sneaking with a magnetized pickaxe.
Ticks between ore-rip pulses. One block per pulse (capped to prevent griefing).
magnetization.configuration.tools.axePul: Chopping a log with a magnetized axe sends a brief radial pull on nearby ferromagnetic items + entities toward the player.
magnetization.configuration.tools.shovel: Digging dirt/sand/gravel/clay/soil with a magnetized shovel may drop trace iron nuggets and rarer raw magnetite.
Probability per shovel-target block break that a trace metal drops.
magnetization.configuration.tools.hoeDow: Right-clicking with a magnetized hoe pings ferromagnetic ore blocks in range with marker particles.
Block radius scanned by the hoe dowsing ping.
magnetization.configuration.debug.toolti: Diagnostic logging toggles. Off by default — only useful when investigating emitter or anchor behavior.
magnetization.configuration.debug.debugL: Master toggle for FieldApplicator + anchor-binding debug logs. Leave off on busy servers; spammy at default.
magnetization.configuration.debug.goggle: Show extra diagnostic lines in goggle/HUD tooltips (e.g. a machine's live kinetic speed / source / network state). Off by default; handy when diagnosing why a block won't power.
magnetization.configuration.commands.too: Required permission level for each /magnetization command group. 0 = any player, 2 = operator (vanilla default), 3-4 = higher op tiers. Read live so /reload picks up edits.
magnetization.configuration.commands.deb: Permission level required to run /magnetization debug field|forceAt.
magnetization.configuration.commands.spa: Permission level required to run /magnetization spawn_test_ship|spawn_test_anchor.
magnetization.configuration.commands.shi: /magnetization clear_phantoms / shatter_all_ships / push_nearest_ship — Required Op Level
Permission level for ship-management utilities. These mutate world state (destroy sub-levels), so the default is operator-only.
magnetization.configuration.commands.lir: Permission level required to run the /magnetization lirm group. Includes lightning summoning; keep op-gated on shared servers.
magnetization.configuration.commands.tpP: Permission level required to run /magnetization tp anomaly|petrified_forest. Biome travel is quality-of-life rather than destructive, so 0 (any player) is the default.
message.magnetization.vector_core_instal: Vector Core installed — the Repulsor now thrusts ships along its facing.
tooltip.magnetization.mr_armor: Magnetorheological fluid armor. In a magnetic field it stays hardened — resists nearly all damage (full set ~95%); otherwise it hardens on a kinetic hit (~90%). Never pulled by fields.
tooltip.magnetization.boots_inert: Inert to magnetic fields until magnetized
tooltip.magnetization.backpack_boost: Glide boost: high-altitude daylight, or the End — no rockets
message.magnetization.sail_night_on: Solar sail: active at night (half power)
tooltip.magnetization.ore_compass.any: Points to the nearest metallic ore vein
hematite_lens.magnetization.tooltip.hint: Right-click in air to toggle N/S. Shift+right-click an emitter to install or remove a polarity lock.
imprint.magnetization.empty: Imprint Module is empty — shift+right-click an emitter to capture.
imprint.magnetization.tooltip.empty_hint: Shift+right-click an emitter to capture its configuration.
imprint.magnetization.tooltip.apply_hint: Shift+right-click another emitter to project this preset onto it.
imprint.magnetization.tooltip.clear_hint: Right-click in air to clear this preset and re-capture.
tooltip.magnetization.iron_oxide_ore.use: Mine for raw material; smelt or blast into an ingot. Magnetic — pulled by attractive fields.
tooltip.magnetization.iron_oxide_storage: Compact storage block. Recompose to 9 raw / ingots. Some variants act as passive emitters — see the Field Manual.
tooltip.magnetization.iron_oxide_ingot.u: Smelting product. Crafts into the matching tier's tools/armor and into the storage block.
tooltip.magnetization.metal_tool.use: Magnetic tool. Place in an electromagnet GUI to stamp polarity; magnetized tools vacuum nearby ferromagnetic items and trigger their category signature ability on use.
tooltip.magnetization.metal_armor.use: Magnetic armor. Place in an electromagnet GUI to stamp polarity; while worn the player becomes a magnetizable entity drawn by fields.
tooltip.magnetization.horse_armor.use: Equip on a horse. Magnetizable in any electromagnet GUI just like wearable armor.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_elytra.us: Wear in chest slot. Glides like vanilla, but the wearer is 4× field-susceptible while gliding — chain emitters to rail-ride between them.
tooltip.magnetization.repulsor_gun.use: Right-click to fire a short-range repulsion cone. Pushes mobs, items, and falling blocks; recoil also launches the holder.
tooltip.magnetization.meteorite_fragment: Rare reagent from breaking a meteorite_core. Used in shortcut recipes for ferromagnetic ingots, lodestone cores, the cosmic compass, and to grow a meteorite sapling or rebuild a fresh core.
tooltip.magnetization.meteorite_core.use: Rare worldgen block that emits a decaying magnetic field. Right-click with any ferromagnetic item to refill.
Plant on solid ground. Matures into a fresh meteorite_core over ~30 in-game minutes (configurable).
tooltip.magnetization.titanomagnetite.em: No field recorded — place next to an active emitter to imprint.
advancement.magnetization.magnets_how_do: Hold a magnetite ingot, a Permanent Magnet, and an Electromagnet at once. (Don't talk to a scientist about it.)
book.magnetization.entry.iron_oxide_fami: Magnetite is the headline iron-oxide ore, but the family runs deeper: $(l)maghemite$() (oxidised surface variant), $(l)pyrrhotite$() (heat-activated iron sulfide), $(l)hematite$() (antiferromagnetic, multiblock role), and $(l)titanomagnetite$() (rare deep-rock, records field exposure).$(br)$(br)All four smelt into ferromagnetic ingots and are tagged for the standard emitter pulls. Their unique mechanics unlock in later releases.

$(l)Maghemite$() $(br)$(br)Reddish iron(III) oxide that forms when magnetite weathers. Drops $(l)raw maghemite$() from ore; smelt for a maghemite ingot. Surface-y Y-band (40..120). Also where your hoarded magnetite ends up — see $(l)oxidation decay$().
$(l)Pyrrhotite$() $(br)$(br)Bronze-tinted iron sulfide. Block form is a $(l)heat-activated emitter$() — see the Pyrrhotite Block entry. Mid-depths Y-band (-32..48).
$(l)Hematite$() $(br)$(br)Dark-red α-Fe₂O₃. Antiferromagnetic — block form is a $(l)field dampener$() (see the Hematite Block entry). Broad Y-band (-40..96). Ingots also craft into red dye.
$(l)Titanomagnetite$() $(br)$(br)Iron-titanium oxide that records the last magnetic field it was exposed to and re-emits it. See the Titanomagnetite Block entry. Scarce deep-rock spawn (-64..-8).

Each ore family ships in the standard set:$(br)$(li)$(o)maghemite_ore$() / $(o)deepslate_maghemite_ore$() / $(o)maghemite_block$() / $(o)raw_maghemite_block$()$(li)$(o)pyrrhotite_ore$() / $(o)deepslate_pyrrhotite_ore$() / $(o)pyrrhotite_block$() / $(o)raw_pyrrhotite_block$()$(li)$(o)hematite_ore$() / $(o)deepslate_hematite_ore$() / $(o)hematite_block$() / $(o)raw_hematite_block$()$(li)$(o)titanomagnetite_ore$() / $(o)deepslate_titanomagnetite_ore$() / $(o)titanomagnetite_block$() / $(o)raw_titanomagnetite_block$()$(br)$(br)Plus the magnetite originals: $(o)magnetite_ore$() / $(o)deepslate_magnetite_ore$() / $(o)magnetite_block$() / $(o)raw_magnetite_block$().

Loose $(l)raw magnetite$() and $(l)magnetite ingots$() in your inventory slowly oxidise. After roughly one Minecraft day of sitting in inventory, each magnetite stack converts in place to its $(l)maghemite$() equivalent — the real-world rusting of Fe$(s)3$()O$(s)4$() to γ-Fe$(s)2$()O$(s)3$() in moist air.$(br)$(br)Tooltip on each stack shows the remaining percent so you can plan ahead. Use the magnetite quickly to keep it; let it sit and you end up maghemite-rich instead.$(br)$(br)Block forms are exempt — your storage doesn't rust. Maghemite itself is terminal (doesn't decay further) and still useful as a weaker-tier tools/armor set.
book.magnetization.entry.anomaly_terrain: Inside the Anomaly biome the very stone is iron-rich, faintly green from oxidised iron veining. The biome's surface is paved in $(l)anomaly stone$() and $(l)magnetic gravel$() — mine, smelt, and build with the full vanilla-stone family of variants.

$(l)Anomaly Stone$()$(br)$(br)Stone variant with iron-oxide veining baked in. Drops cobbled when mined; silk-touch keeps the smooth form. Smelt cobbled back into smooth in a furnace, exactly like vanilla.
$(l)Cobbled Anomaly Stone$()$(br)$(br)The default mining result. Crafts into stairs, slabs, and walls (recipes follow) and can be stonecut for 1:1 yield.

$(l)Magnetic Gravel$()$(br)$(br)Iron-flecked dark gravel. Falls under gravity like normal gravel, and emitters lightly attract it. Vanilla flint is replaced by a small chance of $(l)raw magnetite$() (5%) or $(l)raw maghemite$() (3%) when mined.$(br)$(br)$(o)Worldgen$(): bulk of the Anomaly surface is magnetic gravel, plus very sparse patches in vanilla overworld biomes (~1-in-24 chunks) so survival players who never reach an Anomaly biome can still find a small supply. Disable via $(o)magneticGravelInVanillaBiomes = false$() in the server config.
Rare iron-nickel cores that survive atmospheric entry and bury themselves on the surface, leaving behind a carved $(l)impact crater$() of magnetite, raw_magnetite, and petrified_wood. The core sits at the bowl's centre. Each one emits a powerful magnetic field that decays over ~10 in-game minutes after generation — fresh meteorites pulse at $(l)EXTREME$() strength, faded ones $(l)WEAK$(), and fully-decayed ones go inert.

$(l)Meteorite Core$() $(br)$(br)Worldgen-only — extremely rare overworld surface block embedded in a carved crater. Search for one with a $(l)Cosmic Compass$() (long-range) or a field compass (short-range); the needle will track the decaying signal until it fully fades. Use a ferromagnetic item to right-click and recharge.

Right-click a meteorite core with any ferromagnetic item (magnetite, hematite, raw_iron, etc.) to consume that item and reset the decay timer to full charge. This lets a player keep a found meteorite as a permanent power source — at the cost of an ongoing magnetic-material feed.

If $(l)Applied Energistics 2$() is installed, every AE2 meteor structure also emits a decaying magnetic field at its centre — same decay curve as a native meteorite_core, but with no extra block placed (AE2's sky_stone is undisturbed). A Cosmic Compass will track AE2 meteors right alongside native ones. Server owners can disable this in config: $(l)compat.ae2MeteoriteHookEnabled = false$().
Refined titanomagnetite suspended in an ender-pearl matrix gives you a hand-held version of the titanomagnetite block's field-recording trick. Capture any emitter's exact configuration, walk it across the map, and project the same config onto another emitter — no manual re-tuning.
1 ender pearl in the centre, 4 titanomagnetite ingots in a plus-shape.

$(l)Shift + right-click an active emitter$() while the module is empty to capture its strength, polarity, and range. The tooltip then shows what type of emitter the imprint was taken from.$(br)$(br)$(l)Shift + right-click another emitter$() while the module is filled to project the captured preset onto it. Reusable indefinitely — no charges to track.$(br)$(br)$(l)Right-click in air$() to clear a filled imprint so you can capture a new source. Plain right-click on an emitter is intentionally left alone — that still does the block's normal action (rotate, polarity flip, open GUI). All imprint gestures live on sneak.$(br)$(br)The target emitter clamps projected values to its own GUI ceilings. If you captured an Electromagnet at EXTREME and project onto a Repulsor Coil with a lower max strength, the Repulsor lands at its own ceiling instead of being rejected. Cross-type projection works freely — the tooltip's source line lets you avoid accidental mismatches.
book.magnetization.entry.pyrrhotite_cata: Normally a pyrrhotite block only sees heat on its six $(o)directly adjacent$() faces — touch a blaze burner to one face, the block lights up; one block away, nothing.$(br)$(br)A $(l)Pyrrhotite Catalyst$() relays heat across distance. Touch any heat source to one of the Catalyst's faces, and every pyrrhotite block within the Catalyst's $(l)transmit radius$() (3, 5, or 7 blocks depending on tier) reads that same heat as if it were touching the source itself.

The Catalyst forwards any heat source the pyrrhotite reactor would normally accept directly:$(br)$(li)Create blaze burner — full heat scale$(li)Lava — SEETHING$(li)Fire / soul fire / magma block — KINDLED$(li)Lit campfire / soul campfire — SMOULDERING$(br)$(br)Hover the Catalyst to see what heat tier it's currently bridging.

Catalysts compose freely. Place a row of them and each one independently transmits heat to any pyrrhotite within its own radius — so a single blaze burner can drive a string of pyrrhotite reactors along a long Catalyst spine. Mixed tiers stack naturally: if a pyrrhotite block falls within two Catalysts' radii, the larger one wins.

Three tiers ship: $(l)Basic$() reaches 3 blocks, $(l)Enhanced$() 5, and $(l)Cosmic$() 7. Each tier scans for pyrrhotite within its own radius — mixed-tier networks stack naturally (the largest reach in range wins). Recipes use progressively rarer reagents: Enhanced layers in netherite scrap, Cosmic uses meteorite fragments.
book.magnetization.entry.hematite_lens.i: A hematite-cored polarizer disc that locks an emitter's polarity in place. Once installed, no Polarity Inverter, anomaly biome flux, or other passing field can flip the locked emitter's polarity — it stays exactly where you set it.
book.magnetization.entry.hematite_lens.u: The polarizer itself carries a target polarity (NORTH by default).$(br)$(br)$(l)Right-click in air$() — toggle the polarizer between NORTH and SOUTH.$(br)$(l)Shift + right-click an emitter$() — install the polarizer's current polarity as a lock on that emitter, or remove an existing lock if one is already there.$(br)$(br)Plain right-click on an emitter is intentionally untouched — that still does the block's normal action (rotate, polarity flip, open GUI). All lock gestures live on sneak. The polarizer is reusable — installing or removing a lock doesn't consume it, and the lock survives world reloads.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetic_elytra: A vanilla elytra reforged with ferromagnetic plate and magnetic_plate. Wears in the chest slot like the original; glides like the original. The difference appears in flight: while gliding, the wearer's field-susceptibility multiplies $(4 4×)$, so passing emitters tug them harder than they would on foot.
Vanilla elytra in the centre, magnetic plate above, ferromagnetic ingots in the corners + bottom.

Build a line of permanent magnets or powered emitters along a flight corridor and the elytra wearer can $(l)rail-ride$() between them — NORTH emitters pull, SOUTH emitters push. Stamp the elytra (and any other equipped armor) with a chosen polarity in the electromagnet GUI to decide which emitters attract and which repel.$(br)$(br)Skilled pilots can chain attractive and repulsive emitters to launch themselves cross-map without rockets. Combine with the Imprint Module to deploy matched-config emitter chains quickly. Repair using ferromagnetic ingots.
A Field Compass with a meteorite fragment infused into the casing. The needle scans for the nearest active $(l)meteorite_core$() within 512 blocks and points to it. Fully-decayed meteorites are invisible to the compass — only cores still emitting are tracked.$(br)$(br)Bonus: unlike the Field Compass, the Cosmic Compass is $(l)not$() scrambled by the Magnetic Anomaly biome. Meteorite signals read clean above the biome's flux noise.
book.magnetization.entry.meteorite_sapli: A seed-form of the $(l)meteorite_core$(). Plant one anywhere a sapling could go, leave it alone for about 30 in-game minutes, and it $(l)matures$() into a freshly-charged Meteorite Core with a full EXTREME-strength field.$(br)$(br)Unlike the Core itself, the Sapling drops back to inventory if broken — useful if you planted it in a bad spot.
Craft a sapling from one Meteorite Fragment cradled in four raw magnetite.

$(l)Meteorite Sapling$()$(br)$(br)Place on any solid block; it grows on its own as long as the chunk stays loaded. No light, water, or sky access required — the sapling consumes its own ferromagnetic mass.
maghemite.magnetization.oxidized: Your magnetite has oxidized into maghemite.
compass.magnetization.anomaly_empty: Compass spinning wildly — anomalous field flux.
lirm.magnetization.struck: Lightning Induced Remnant Magnetism — gear polarised %s.
tooltip.magnetization.tool_signature.swo: Magnetized: hits yank opposite-pole armored targets toward you.
tooltip.magnetization.tool_signature.pic: Magnetized: sneak to slowly rip nearby ferromagnetic ore out of the world.
tooltip.magnetization.tool_signature.axe: Magnetized: chopping a log pulls nearby ferromagnetic drops toward you.
tooltip.magnetization.tool_signature.sho: Magnetized: chance to pan trace iron / raw magnetite from loose earth.
tooltip.magnetization.tool_signature.hoe: Magnetized: sneak + right-click to dowse for ferromagnetic ore in range.
gui.magnetization.redstone_fuel_slot: Redstone power. Any redstone dust here keeps the emitter active — no external signal required.
gui.magnetization.tool_slot: Enchanted tool or book. Its enchantments (Fortune, Silk Touch, etc.) apply to drops the emitter pulls in.
gui.magnetization.armor_slot: Armor or metal tool to magnetize. The polarity buttons stamp this item with a pole.
tooltip.magnetization.electromagnet.use: Powered by redstone. Pulls nearby ships and ferromagnetic items.
tooltip.magnetization.kinetic_electromag: Powered by Create rotation. Strength scales with RPM.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_anchor.us: Locks the first ship that enters its range. Persists across reload.
tooltip.magnetization.repulsor_coil.use: Pushes ships outward in a 45° cone along its facing. Place upward for a hover pad; place sideways or down to line a propulsion tunnel.
tooltip.magnetization.tractor_beam.use: Directional pull along facing. Wrench to rotate.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_excavator: Powered by redstone (external signal or internal redstone slot). Projects a widening cone along its facing and continuously pulls every ferromagnetic block in range — each pulled block tunnels through obstructions toward the emitter, drops on arrival, and feeds adjacent inventories. Wrench to rotate; right-click for the GUI.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_plate.use: Flat ferromagnetic plate. Used as cladding.
tooltip.magnetization.field_compass.use: Right-click for the strongest field. Sneak right-click lists every active field in range.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_grapple.u: Right-click to launch toward the nearest attractive emitter (24-block range). Sneak mid-flight to cancel.
jei.magnetization.ferromagnetic.info: Items in this list are pulled by Magnetization emitters when dropped on the ground. Other mods can extend the list via the #magnetization:ferromagnetic tag.
jei.magnetization.ferromagnetic_blocks.i: Blocks in this list are ripped out of the ground by the Magnetic Excavator. Anything in their path gets pulled along too. Other mods extend the list via the #magnetization:ferromagnetic_blocks tag.
rei.magnetization.ferromagnetic.name: Ferromagnetic items
rei.magnetization.ferromagnetic.info: Items in this list are pulled by Magnetization emitters when dropped on the ground. Other mods can extend the list via the #magnetization:ferromagnetic tag.
rei.magnetization.ferromagnetic_blocks.i: Blocks in this list are ripped out of the ground by the Magnetic Excavator. Anything in their path gets pulled along too. Other mods extend the list via the #magnetization:ferromagnetic_blocks tag.
emi.magnetization.ferromagnetic.info: Items in this list are pulled by Magnetization emitters when dropped on the ground. Other mods can extend the list via the #magnetization:ferromagnetic tag.
emi.magnetization.ferromagnetic_blocks.i: Blocks in this list are ripped out of the ground by the Magnetic Excavator. Anything in their path gets pulled along too. Other mods extend the list via the #magnetization:ferromagnetic_blocks tag.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_switch.us: Emits a redstone signal proportional to nearest ship's distance (8-block range).
tooltip.magnetization.permanent_magnet.u: Always-on weak field. Right-click flips polarity. Build propulsion tracks by lining up opposing magnets between world and ship.
tooltip.magnetization.temporary_magnet.u: Cheap, decaying counterpart. Emits a weak field for 10 minutes after placement, then reverts to an Iron Block (redstone consumed at craft is not returned). Use for short-lived propulsion segments.
Place adjacent to an emitter to flip its field polarity, or anywhere on a contraption to flip the whole ship's polarity. Parity: two inverters cancel.
tooltip.magnetization.raw_magnetite.use: Mined from magnetite ore. Smelt for an ingot.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetite_ore.use: Drops raw magnetite. Fortune multiplies the drop.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetite_block.us: Compact storage for magnetite ingots. Ferromagnetic in bulk.
advancement.magnetization.root.title: Magnetic North
advancement.magnetization.root.descripti: Craft a Lodestone Core — the heart of every emitter.
Pick up your first magnetic emitter.
advancement.magnetization.full_kit.title: Aeromagnetic Engineer
advancement.magnetization.full_kit.descr: Hold every emitter in the addon at once.
advancement.magnetization.find_magnetite: Mine raw magnetite (Fe₃O₄) from a magnetite ore.
advancement.magnetization.smelt_magnetit: Smelt raw magnetite into a magnetite ingot.
advancement.magnetization.full_loadout.d: Hold a magnetite and ferromagnetic sword and pickaxe.
advancement.magnetization.dual_magnetize: Carry a north-stamped and a south-stamped piece of magnetized armor.
advancement.magnetization.vein_whisperer: Hold a Magnetic Excavator. Mining without lifting a finger.
advancement.magnetization.inverted_quarr: Wrench a Magnetic Excavator to point it where the ore is.
advancement.magnetization.crafted_repuls: Craft a Repulsor Gun — the inverse of the Magnetic Grapple.
Launch yourself by firing the Repulsor Gun at a magnetic emitter.
advancement.magnetization.powered_by_ene: Drive a magnetic emitter with FE/RF energy.
book.magnetization.field_manual.name: Field Manual
book.magnetization.field_manual.subtitle: A Magnetization addon guide
book.magnetization.field_manual.landing: Magnetization adds magnetism to Create: Aeronautics. This book walks through the materials, emitter blocks, ship polarity, and advanced systems. Tap a category on the right to jump in.
book.magnetization.category.basics.name: Basics
book.magnetization.category.basics.desc: Materials, fields, and the language of poles.
book.magnetization.category.emitters.des: Blocks that produce a magnetic field.
book.magnetization.category.ships.name: Ships
book.magnetization.category.advanced.des: Magnetized gear, LIRM, custom biomes, tools.
book.magnetization.entry.intro.name: Welcome
book.magnetization.entry.intro.page1.tex: Every block in this addon either $(l)emits a magnetic field$() or $(l)responds to one$(). Fields have a $(6)polarity$() ($(3)NORTH$() or $(c)SOUTH$()), a strength tier ($(o)WEAK→EXTREME$()), and a shape (omnidirectional, directional, conical).$(br)Like polarities $(c)repel$(); opposite polarities $(3)attract$(). That's the entire rule.
book.magnetization.entry.intro.page2.tex: Fields push or pull:$(br)$(li)$(l)Sable sub-levels$() — Create: Aeronautics ships intersecting the field. Big ships rotate naturally because closer ends feel stronger pull.$(li)$(l)Magnetizable entities$() — iron golems, undead mobs, ferromagnetic item drops, players in metal armor.$(br)$(br)Onboard-only setups produce zero net thrust — internal forces cancel, just like real physics. To propel a ship, build $(o)between$() the world and the ship.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetite.name: Magnetite
book.magnetization.entry.magnetite.page1: $(item)Magnetite$() (Fe$(0)3$()O$(0)4$()) is the addon's signature ore. Found at $(l)y=-32 to 16$() in the overworld, mined with an iron pickaxe. Smelt raw magnetite into ingots, then craft into plates and the Lodestone Core that every emitter needs.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetite.spotl: Drops $(l)1–2$() raw magnetite per ore (Fortune compatible). Renewable from the Magnetic Excavator.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetite.smelt: Furnace or Blast Furnace, your choice.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetite.plate: Foundational crafting reagent. Most emitters need 4–8 of these.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetite.core: The Lodestone Core is the 'magnet' inside every emitter — keep some on hand.
book.magnetization.entry.polarity.name: Polarity
Every field, every magnetized armor piece, every ship carries a $(3)NORTH$() or $(c)SOUTH$() pole. The math is symmetric: a NORTH emitter pulls a SOUTH target the same way a SOUTH emitter pulls a NORTH one.$(br)$(br)Default convention: when a target's pole is unspecified, treat it as $(3)NORTH$(). That means a freshly-placed magnet will $(c)repel$() an untagged ship until you flip one of them.
Place an $(item)Polarity Inverter$() next to an emitter to flip the emitter's field. Two adjacent inverters cancel (parity).$(br)$(br)Place an inverter $(l)anywhere on a contraption$() to flip the whole ship's polarity (also parity-counted across the contraption — see Ship Polarity).
book.magnetization.entry.electromagnet.s: Redstone-powered field emitter. Right-click to open the tuning GUI: pick polarity, strength, range. Active while powered.
book.magnetization.entry.electromagnet.g: The same GUI also stamps polarity onto magnetized armor and tools placed in its slot. The stamp is permanent unless cleared.$(br)$(br)$(l)Sneak + right-click an emitter with a wrench$() (anything tagged $(o)c:tools/wrench$(), including Create's) to reset every override — strength, range, polarity, ship binding — back to defaults. Plain right-click with a wrench still rotates the block.
book.magnetization.entry.kinetic_electro: Create-kinetic-driven variant of the Electromagnet. Spin direction sets polarity; RPM scales strength. Works in contraptions.
book.magnetization.entry.anchor.name: Magnetic Anchor
book.magnetization.entry.anchor.spotligh: A long-range pull-only emitter. Bind it to a specific ship by sneak-right-clicking that ship while holding the anchor item.
A bound anchor only pulls its target ship — other ships in range are ignored. Useful for parking spots, docking pads, and tracked-shuttle infrastructure.
book.magnetization.entry.tractor.name: Tractor Beam
book.magnetization.entry.tractor.spotlig: Directional pull along the placement axis. Hits everything in a thin cone. Great for vacuuming distant ships into a landing pad.
book.magnetization.entry.repulsor.name: Repulsor Coil
book.magnetization.entry.repulsor.spotli: Short-range $(c)repulsive$() conical field. Default range $(l)8 blocks$(). Tile across a tunnel floor for a hover-track, or line a chute to shove ships through.
book.magnetization.entry.excavator.name: Magnetic Excavator
book.magnetization.entry.excavator.spotl: Rips ferromagnetic blocks out of the ground and yanks them to itself. Each ripped block becomes a Sable sub-level briefly. Wrench to point.
Either feed it a redstone signal, or stash a redstone item in its internal slot (any block/dust/etc. that emits or contains redstone). Items in the slot are never consumed — presence is what matters.
book.magnetization.entry.permanent.name: Permanent / Temporary Magnets
book.magnetization.entry.permanent.spotl: Always-on weak field. Right-click flips polarity. Use these to line propulsion tracks between world and ship.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetic_switch: Proximity sensor for ships. Emits a redstone signal proportional to the nearest Sable sub-level within an 8-block radius. Use it to gate doors, drop landing pads, or trigger lighting as a ship approaches.

Signal scales from $(l)15$() at point-blank to $(l)0$() at the edge of the 8-block range. Pair with a comparator for finer control. Updates every 4 ticks, so the switch is cheap to leave running on any chunk.
book.magnetization.entry.permanent.tempo: The Temporary Magnet is cheaper but reverts to an Iron Block after 10 minutes. Good for short-lived propulsion segments.
book.magnetization.entry.ship_polarity.p: Every Sable sub-level (Create: Aeronautics ship) has a derived magnetic state:$(br)$(li)$(3)Polarity$() — $(3)NORTH$() by default. Each Polarity Inverter aboard flips it (parity: 1 = $(c)SOUTH$(), 2 = $(3)NORTH$() again).$(li)$(o)Susceptibility$() — baseline + per-ferrous-block + per-magnet-block bonus, capped. Iron-heavy ships respond more strongly.

$(l)Magnet blocks on the ship contribute to susceptibility only$(), never to polarity. This is deliberate: flipping a mounted electromagnet's pole for ship-to-ship combat must not accidentally invert your own ship and tear the contraption apart.$(br)$(br)Goggles, Jade, WTHIT, and TOP all surface a ship's current pole and susceptibility when you look at an emitter on it.
book.magnetization.entry.propulsion.name: Propulsion
book.magnetization.entry.propulsion.page: Line $(item)Permanent Magnets$() in the world with opposing magnets on a ship — the alternation produces a continuous push/pull along the track. Think a real-world maglev rail.
$(item)Repulsor Coils$() tiled across a tunnel floor work as a one-way pusher. Cheap, no kinetic needed, and you can chain them as long as you want.
Pair of anchors bound to the same ship will cooperatively damp its angular velocity until it settles level. A single anchor pulls; two share the spin-bleed work.
book.magnetization.entry.armor_tools.pag: Place any armor piece tagged $(o)#magnetization:metal_armor$() in an electromagnet GUI to stamp it with a polarity. Vanilla iron / gold / chain / netherite all qualify, plus the addon's three tiers:$(br)$(li)$(l)Maghemite$() — rusted-magnetite tier (defense 1/4/3/1), entry-level$(li)$(l)Magnetite$() — iron-equivalent (defense 2/6/5/2), the standard$(li)$(l)Ferromagnetic$() — alloy tier (defense 3/8/6/3), endgame$(br)$(br)Wearing magnetized armor makes you a magnetizable entity — nearby fields pull you, and a held magnetized tool also vacuums dropped ferromagnetic items.

Same three tiers exist for swords/pickaxes/axes/shovels/hoes. Each magnetized tool gets a signature ability on top of the shared item-vacuum:$(br)$(li)$(l)Sword$() — yank nearby entities toward you on hit.$(li)$(l)Pickaxe$() — rip neighbouring ferromagnetic ore on break.$(li)$(l)Axe$() — pulse-knockback enemies on attack.$(li)$(l)Shovel$() — chance to pan magnetite nuggets when digging.$(li)$(l)Hoe$() — dowsing ping reveals ferromagnetic blocks nearby.$(br)$(br)Vanilla iron / gold / netherite tools in $(o)#magnetization:metal_tools$() qualify too — magnetize an iron sword and it picks up all the same signature behaviours.

Horse body armor counts as armor for magnetism purposes — equip a horse with magnetite or ferromagnetic horse armor and the horse becomes a magnetizable entity. Stamp polarity in any electromagnet GUI just like wearable armor. Magnetite horse armor matches iron in defense; ferromagnetic matches diamond.
7 maghemite ingots in a U-shape. Below iron in defense — fits the rusted-magnetite flavour. Still tagged for magnetism so a stamped horse drifts in fields.
7 ingots in a U-shape. Iron-tier protection (armor 5) for your horse.
Same U-shape with ferromagnetic ingots. Diamond-tier protection (armor 11).
book.magnetization.entry.lirm.name: Lightning-Induced Remnant Magnetism
book.magnetization.entry.lirm.page1.text: A lightning strike on a player wearing metal armor / tools imparts a $(l)temporary$() magnetization (LIRM). The polarity stamp decays linearly over 20 minutes, then auto-clears.$(br)$(br)Free magnetism — but watch the timer. Permanent stamps via the Electromagnet GUI do not decay.
book.magnetization.entry.biomes.name: Custom Biomes
book.magnetization.entry.biomes.page1.te: Rare cold/dry inland biome. Compasses spin wildly inside. Fields get a $(l)1.5×$() strength bonus here — build labs and arena PvP zones here for amped effects.$(br)$(br)$(o)/magnetization tp anomaly$() to scout.
book.magnetization.entry.biomes.page2.te: Rare biome covered in petrified wood. Lightning strikes on petrified logs may magnetize the strike point. Petrified wood is weakly ferromagnetic.$(br)$(br)$(o)/magnetization tp petrified_forest$() to scout.
book.magnetization.entry.compass_grapple: Points at the nearest active emitter in range. Shift+right-click for an entity-locked variant. Curios-slot compatible.
Right-click an attractive emitter to zip toward it. Default range $(l)24 blocks$(). Curios-slot compatible.
magnetization.configuration.items.repuls: Cone range in blocks. Shorter than the Magnetic Grapple's scan because the gun is crowd-control / self-launch, not long-range traversal.
Cosine of the cone half-angle. 0.866 = 30°. Narrower than the Repulsor Coil's 45° so aim rewards.
Velocity (blocks/tick) the gun pushes the holder back when aiming at a magnetic emitter block. Linear falloff over the cone's range. Set 0 to disable self-recoil entirely.
Game-ticks between shots (20 = 1s). Prevents spam-bouncing exploits while still allowing rapid follow-ups.

Base linear impulse (Sable kg·m/s) applied to a ship per shot. Velocity change = impulse / ship-mass, so small ships fly farther. Default 40 ≈ 16 m/s on a 1-block test cube (capped) and ~0.4 m/s on a 100-block contraption.
Per-shot cap on the velocity change a ship can receive (m/s). Stops tiny test ships from launching across the world. Default 8 m/s ≈ a couple of blocks of forward drift per cap-clamped shot.
magnetization.configuration.items.cosmic: Maximum distance (blocks) the Cosmic Compass scans for an active meteorite_core. Default 512 — generous because meteorites are rare and the compass's whole job is finding them. Larger values increase per-tick scan cost slightly.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.met: Ticks between a fresh meteorite's full-charge state and full inertness. Default 12000 = 10 in-game minutes. The strength tier steps EXTREME → STRONG → WEAK → inert as the timer elapses. Right-click with any ferromagnetic item to reset.

Ticks the Meteorite Sapling takes to mature into a fresh meteorite_core. Default 36000 = 30 in-game minutes. Adjust to taste; faster makes sapling-farming trivial, slower makes it a long-term project.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.mag: How far the Magnetic Switch scans for a Sable ship/contraption to derive its redstone output strength. Default 8 blocks. Larger ranges let one switch cover a hangar; smaller ranges force tight placement.
magnetization.configuration.worldgen.ae2: If true and Applied Energistics 2 is installed, every AE2 meteor structure also emits a decaying magnetic field at its centre (same decay curve as our native meteorite_core, no extra block placed). Disable to skip the per-chunk AE2 scan if you don't want the cross-mod integration.
book.magnetization.entry.pyrrhotite_bloc: A full block of pyrrhotite is a $(l)heat-activated magnetic emitter$(). On its own it's inert; place a heat source on any of its six faces and the lattice's iron sulfide dipoles align, broadcasting a $(l)NORTH$()-polarity omnidirectional field whose strength rises with the heat level.

Create's $(l)Blaze Burner$() is the canonical heat source; mapping is: $(br)$(br)$(l)Smouldering$() / $(l)Fading$() → WEAK $(br)$(l)Kindled$() → STRONG $(br)$(l)Seething$() → EXTREME $(br)$(br)No adjacent heat = no field. To flip polarity, stack a $(l)Polarity Inverter$() next to the block.

Direct adjacency works, but most setups use a $(l)Pyrrhotite Catalyst$() to bridge heat across a few blocks — see the Catalyst entry. Mixed-tier Catalyst networks stack (largest reach in range wins). Tooltip on the block always reports the current observed heat tier so you can debug a chain visually.
A full block of hematite is a $(l)passive dampener$(). It emits no field of its own — α-Fe₂O₃ is antiferromagnetic, every dipole pair cancels — but placed next to any active emitter, each adjacent hematite face drops the emitter's effective strength tier by one step.

Strength steps down sequentially: $(br)$(br)EXTREME $(o)→$() STRONG $(o)→$() WEAK $(br)$(br)WEAK is the clamp floor — adding more hematite faces past that point has no further effect. Use it to throttle an over-powered emitter without rewiring its GUI, or to surround a polarity-locked emitter with a soft falloff zone.
book.magnetization.entry.titanomagnetite: A full block of titanomagnetite is a $(l)field recorder$(). Place it within a few blocks of any active emitter; on its next tick it captures the strongest neighbouring field (strength, polarity, shape) into its block-entity and starts re-emitting that field from its own position.$(br)$(br)Remove the source emitter and the titanomagnetite keeps emitting indefinitely. Re-expose to a stronger field to overwrite the imprint. The record persists across world reloads and server restarts.

Hover the block: the tooltip shows $(l)Recorded: STRONG NORTH$() (or similar) once a field has been captured, or an empty-state hint before that. The block needs $(l)no power$() — its emission is passive, driven by the captured imprint.$(br)$(br)Break and replace the block to wipe the imprint. For a portable version, craft an $(l)Imprint Module$() — same paleomagnetic trick, hand-held.
The mod's universal crafting filler — a flat plate of magnetised material that shows up in nearly every advanced recipe (Permanent Magnet, Temporary Magnet, Magnetic Anchor, Polarizer, etc.). Cheap to produce in bulk.
3 magnetite ingots in a horizontal row → 1 magnetic plate. Repeat freely; most setups want a stack on hand.
book.magnetization.entry.ferromagnetic_i: Alloy ingot that mixes magnetite with iron + lodestone for endgame-tier recipes. Used in the top-tier armor + tools, the Repulsor Gun, Magnetic Grapple, and most field-shaping blocks. Tagged $(o)#magnetization:ferromagnetic$() so it's pulled by attractive fields.

1 lodestone_core in the centre, magnetite ingots on the four edges, iron ingots on the four corners → 3 ferromagnetic ingots. Cheaper shortcut: $(l)1 meteorite_fragment + 4 magnetite$() (plus-shape) → 8 ferromagnetic ingots, but requires a meteorite hunt first.
Pre-charged lodestone — the heart of every advanced field-handling block (Magnetic Anchor, Magnetic Grapple, etc.). Strongly ferromagnetic on its own. Equivalent of vanilla netherite's role for magnetism: rare, focal, only used where it's structurally needed.
8 magnetic plates around a centre lodestone → 1 lodestone core. The plates form the dipole shell; the lodestone in the middle anchors the polarity field.
book.magnetization.entry.meteorite_fragm: Rare reagent dropped (1–3 per break) when you mine a $(l)meteorite_core$(). Pre-magnetised at the molecular level — every fragment carries traces of the parent meteorite's decaying field. Used as a shortcut catalyst in two recipes:
1 fragment + 4 magnetite (plus-shape) → 8 ferromagnetic ingots. Skips the lodestone-core gating of the standard recipe; per-fragment yield is higher but you need to actually find a meteorite first.
1 fragment + 1 magnetic_plate (shapeless) → 4 lodestone cores. The fragment substitutes for the 8-plate spiral of the standard recipe — flavoured as the meteorite already carrying the field.
book.magnetization.entry.fe_rf.name: FE/RF Power
book.magnetization.entry.fe_rf.page1.tex: Every redstone-powered emitter — Electromagnet, Magnetic Anchor, Repulsor Coil, Tractor Beam, Magnetic Excavator — also accepts $(l)FE/RF$() from any energy-providing mod (Create: Crafts & Additions, Mekanism, Thermal, Immersive Engineering generators, Applied Energistics). No specific dependency needed; FE is built into NeoForge.
book.magnetization.entry.fe_rf.page2.tex: Each emitter has an internal $(l)50,000 FE$() buffer (default). Connect any FE source — wires, cables, generators — and the buffer fills.$(br)$(br)$(l)Redstone takes priority$(): if the emitter is receiving a redstone signal, that drives it for free. Without redstone, the emitter consumes $(l)10 FE/tick$() from its buffer while emitting. The GUI's right-side bar shows current fill and active source.
book.magnetization.entry.fe_rf.page3.tex: Server owners can lock players out of either source via $(o)compat.allowRedstonePower$() and $(o)compat.allowEnergyPower$(). Setting redstone to false makes every emitter require an FE supply — meaningful since redstone is effectively infinite once a lever is placed. Set both true (default) to let players pick.
book.magnetization.entry.repulsor_gun.sp: Hand-held conical repulsor. Right-click to fire a single pulse in your look direction. Cooldown-gated like the Magnetic Grapple — no fuel.
book.magnetization.entry.repulsor_gun.ef: $(li)$(l)Ships$() in the cone get pushed away.$(li)$(l)Magnetized entities$() (mobs in magnetized armor, or carrying the Magnetized effect) get knockback.$(li)$(l)Dropped items and falling blocks$() get nudged out of the cone — defensive crowd-clearing trick.
book.magnetization.entry.repulsor_gun.cu: If your shot lands on a $(l)magnetic emitter block$() (lodestone, electromagnet, permanent magnet, etc.), you get $(l)kicked back$() along the inverse of your aim — closer magnet = harder push. The headline traversal trick: aim down at a lodestone to launch yourself upward and backward.$(br)$(br)Curios-compatible: drop the gun in a charm slot and bind $(o)Use Repulsor Gun$() under $(l)Options → Controls → Key Binds → Magnetization$().
book.magnetization.entry.repulsor_gun.re: One Magnetic Plate, one Lodestone Core, one Ferromagnetic Ingot, and three iron ingots. The plate shapes the cone, the core anchors the field, the ingot stiffens the recoil bracket.
book.magnetization.entry.halbach.name: Halbach Array
book.magnetization.entry.halbach.spotlig: Line up magnets of the $(l)same polarity$() so their faces touch and the field concentrates. Each face-adjacent same-pole magnet steps an emitter's strength tier $(l)up$() (clamped to EXTREME) — a row of aligned magnets reads as one powerful emitter. The exact mirror of the hematite dampener.
book.magnetization.entry.emp_charge.name: EMP Flux Charge
book.magnetization.entry.emp_charge.spot: A single-use electromagnetic bomb. Power it with redstone and it $(c)detonates$(): every magnetic emitter in a $(l)12-block radius$() goes dark for 10s and every machine/battery's stored FE is wiped, with a flash + thunderclap. Fries electronics, not terrain.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetostrictiv: A buried magnetic tripwire. Nearby motion — a player or mob running/jumping within $(l)8 blocks$() — shifts its $(l)magnetoresistance$() and it emits an $(l)analog redstone$() signal (stronger the closer/faster), decaying when things go still. Detects through blocks: an invisible perimeter alarm.
book.magnetization.entry.barkhausen.name: Barkhausen Noise Generator
book.magnetization.entry.barkhausen.spot: A true random-number block. With a magnet placed against it, its iron domains flip chaotically and it emits a rapid, $(l)unpredictable$() stream of random redstone strengths; idle otherwise. For random lighting, casino logic, or unpredictable spawners.
book.magnetization.entry.kinetic_coil.sp: Faraday's law as a generator: a magnetic ship moving through the coil induces an EMF proportional to its speed, so the coil $(l)generates FE$() (pushed to adjacent machines) and emits a redstone pulse while the magnet passes. No fuel — pure kinetic-to-electric.
book.magnetization.entry.induction_pad.s: Buffers FE from cables/generators and $(l)wirelessly tops up$() every energy-storing item a nearby player carries — main inventory, armor, offhand, and Curios slots. Electromagnetic induction, gamified.
Mount it on a craft and power it — it magnetically $(l)locks the ship's orientation$(), cancelling angular velocity each tick so the craft moves but won't spin, pitch, or roll. Unpowered, it rotates freely. Hold a heading or run cleanly over repulsor tracks.
book.magnetization.entry.structural_indu: Place it under a structure and power it: it grabs the whole solid mass directly above, glues it into a single $(l)physics craft$(), and lifts it free. Gated on clearance above (won't grind into a ceiling); skips block entities, fluids, and protected blocks.
book.magnetization.entry.vector_core.spo: Craft a $(l)Vector Core$() (titanomagnetite chip) and right-click a Repulsor Coil to install it. The coil then $(l)thrusts$() magnetic ships along its facing — a self-propelling conveyor/track — on top of its normal repulsion, up to a terminal track speed.
book.magnetization.entry.lenz.name: Lenz-Effect Braking
book.magnetization.entry.lenz.spotlight: A magnetic ship moving over conductive non-ferrous blocks ($(l)eddy conductors$() — copper, aluminium…) is dragged to a slow float by induced eddy currents, exactly like a magnet falling through a copper pipe. Lay a copper track to brake or gently lower a craft.
book.magnetization.entry.homopolar_motor: A Create kinetic $(l)generator that runs on a magnet alone$() — no fuel, no energy. Open its GUI and drop a magnet in; it spins a shaft on its placement axis. Speed + stress capacity scale with the magnet, but it's deliberately gentle — a compact starter generator.
book.magnetization.entry.mhd_jet.name: MHD Jet Thruster
book.magnetization.entry.mhd_jet.spotlig: A magnetohydrodynamic engine — the mod's $(l)strongest$() ship drive. Needs $(l)both$() a magnet (right-click to slot) $(l)and FE$(): the magnet sets the speed ceiling + thrust, and a stronger magnet burns proportionally more FE, so big magnets only reach full speed with matching power.
The $(l)top$() propulsion tier (capillary spike jet). Burns $(l)raw ferrofluid$() (an 8-bucket internal tank, topped up with ferrofluid buckets) plus FE to push a craft harder and faster than anything else. Stored fluid + FE show in WTHIT / Jade / The One Probe.
book.magnetization.entry.solar_sail.name: Magnetosphere Solar Sail
book.magnetization.entry.solar_sail.spot: Mount these panels on an airship for $(l)passive, fuel-free$() forward thrust — they catch the solar wind. Build a wide one-deep wall: thrust scales with $(l)panel count$() and $(l)altitude$(). Full by day, half by night — right-click to toggle a night cut-off.
book.magnetization.entry.alfven_backpack: A chest-slot $(l)Elytra alternative$(). Its superconducting ribbons give a permanent, fuel-free forward boost while gliding — $(l)no firework rockets$() — when you're high up in daylight or anywhere in the End.
book.magnetization.entry.ferrofluid.name: Ferrofluid
book.magnetization.entry.ferrofluid.spot: A black, water-like magnetic liquid. Generates naturally $(l)only in the Magnetic Anomaly biome$() (its water bodies are ferrofluid); otherwise player-made (water bucket + 2 raw magnetite). Plain ferrofluid is $(l)field-immune$() — it sits still and reacts to nothing on its own.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetized_ferr: Drop a ferrofluid bucket in the $(l)electromagnet GUI$() and pick a pole to magnetize it. Pour it out and it's a $(l)flowing fluid that also emits a weak field$(), pulling items and ships with that polarity. Magnetized fluid flowing into plain ferrofluid converts it — $(l)magnetization takes priority$().
book.magnetization.entry.mr_fluid.name: Magnetorheological Fluid
book.magnetization.entry.mr_fluid.spotli: An iron-particle smart fluid. It flows like a liquid, but inside a $(l)magnetic field$() it instantly stiffens into $(l)Hardened MR Fluid$() — a solid block you can walk on — and melts back when the field is gone. Field-toggled bridges, gates, and walkways.
book.magnetization.entry.tokamak.name: Tokamak Fusion Reactor
book.magnetization.entry.tokamak.spotlig: Ring a $(l)Tokamak Reactor Core$() with 8 Confinement Coils, then right-click the core with a $(l)Deuterium Cell$(). Ringed + fuelled, it fuses and generates $(l)2,000 FE/tick$() — the mod's top-tier power source. Fuel is real D-T: deuterium from a water bucket in a containment shell.
book.magnetization.entry.ore_compass.spo: A dowsing rod for $(l)metallic ore veins$() — it points to the nearest one. Combine it with a specific metallic ore in an anvil to $(l)tune$() it to track only that ore instead of any.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetic_item_f: A thin magnetic wall plate that holds one item, tool, or armor stuck flat to it for display (no rotation). Right-click to stick on, empty-hand to pop off. Crafted from a vanilla item frame + a magnetic plate.
book.magnetization.entry.diamagnetic.spo: $(l)Pyrolytic Carbon$() (4 charcoal) is strongly diamagnetic. Dropped within a magnetic field it's repelled by $(l)both$() poles — pushed away with its motion damped — so it settles into a stable hover above a magnet instead of being pulled in. Real diamagnetic levitation.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetic_anvils: Anvils cast from our metals — full vanilla anvil behaviour, but $(l)self-dampened$() (no clang) with per-metal durability. Magnetite/maghemite/hematite are convenient-but-degrading; $(l)Titanomagnetite never degrades$() — the only metal that beats a vanilla anvil + dampener.
book.magnetization.entry.mr_armor.name: Magnetorheological Armor
book.magnetization.entry.mr_armor.spotli: Iron-light, flexible armor infused with magnetorheological fluid. Inside a $(l)magnetic field$() it stays permanently rigid — shrugging off $(l)nearly all damage$() (full set ≈ 95%) and it's never pulled by the field. Out of a field it instead snaps rigid the instant a $(l)kinetic hit$() lands (physical/fall/blast), scaling with pieces worn (≈ 90%). Made by infusing iron armor with MR fluid.
book.magnetization.entry.deuterium_oxide: Heavy water — water whose hydrogen is the heavier $(l)deuterium$() isotope. Made by irradiating a bucket of water with $(l)glowstone dust$(). It behaves just like water (a touch denser, a darker blue), and is the fuel the $(l)Deuterium Fuel Cell$() runs on.
An iron-golem-like guardian bodied in magnetorheological fluid. Crafted as a $(l)spawn egg$() (four MR fluid buckets around a carved pumpkin) since you can't stack a fluid into a multiblock. A little less sturdy than an iron golem, but the fluid soaks impacts — and inside a $(l)magnetic field$() it locks rigid, becoming $(l)near-immune to damage$() and immovable.
book.magnetization.entry.magnetoresistiv: Magnetic-metal boots that $(l)auto-save you from any fall$() (costing a little durability per save). Uniquely, they're $(l)inert to fields$() — never yanked by emitters — until you magnetize them in the electromagnet GUI, after which they react like other magnetized gear.
book.magnetization.entry.g_force_cushion: Place it at the bottom of a drop: anyone wearing $(l)metallic armor$() who lands on it has their fall completely arrested — zero fall damage from any height. Without metallic armor (the conductor the field grips), it's a normal landing.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_item_fram: Mount on any face. Right-click an item to display it; power it (redstone/FE) to spin it. Right-click or wrench to flip spin; sneak to remove.
tooltip.magnetization.induction_pad.use: Feed it FE; it wirelessly tops up energy items a nearby player carries — inventory, armor, offhand, Curios.
tooltip.magnetization.kinetic_coil.use: A magnetic ship passing through generates FE (to adjacent machines) and a redstone pulse. No fuel.
tooltip.magnetization.emp_charge.use: Redstone-detonated. Darkens every emitter and wipes stored FE within 12 blocks. Single use.
tooltip.magnetization.gyrostabilizer.use: Mount on a ship and power it to lock its orientation — moves freely but won't spin or tip.
tooltip.magnetization.g_force_cushion.us: Place at the bottom of a drop. Wearing metallic armor, you land with zero fall damage from any height.
tooltip.magnetization.solar_sail.use: Mount on an airship for passive forward thrust in daylight — scales with panel count + altitude. Right-click toggles night cut-off; wrench rotates.
tooltip.magnetization.mhd_jet.use: Ship engine: slot a magnet (sets speed ceiling) and feed FE. Stronger magnet needs more power. Wrench rotates.
tooltip.magnetization.micro_thruster.use: Top-tier ship engine: fill with ferrofluid buckets + feed FE for extreme thrust. Wrench rotates.
tooltip.magnetization.homopolar_motor.us: Right-click to open; drop a magnet in to spin a Create shaft. No fuel — output scales with the magnet.
tooltip.magnetization.structural_inducer: Power it to grab the structure in front of its face and launch it as a physics craft. Wrench rotates; right-click sets range.
tooltip.magnetization.tokamak_controller: Ring it with 8 Tokamak Coils, then right-click with a Deuterium Cell. Fuses for a large, steady FE output.
tooltip.magnetization.tokamak_coil.use: Confinement coil — ring 8 of these around a Tokamak Reactor Core to form the reactor.
tooltip.magnetization.deuterium_cell.use: Fusion fuel. Right-click a fuelled Tokamak Reactor Core to load it (~4 minutes of fusion per cell).
tooltip.magnetization.magnetostrictive_s: Emits analog redstone when a player or mob moves within 8 blocks — an invisible perimeter alarm.
tooltip.magnetization.barkhausen.use: With a magnet placed against it, emits a stream of random redstone strengths — a true RNG.
tooltip.magnetization.vector_core.use: Right-click a Repulsor Coil to install it — the coil then thrusts ships along its facing.
tooltip.magnetization.pyrolytic_carbon.u: Strongly diamagnetic. Dropped in a magnetic field it's repelled by both poles and hovers above a magnet.
tooltip.magnetization.mr_fluid.use: Magnetorheological fluid: flows like a liquid, but hardens into a walkable solid inside a magnetic field.
tooltip.magnetization.petrified_wood.use: Weakly ferromagnetic wood from the Petrified Forest — faintly tugged by fields.
tooltip.magnetization.magnetic_anvil.use: A self-dampened anvil (no clang). Per-metal durability — titanomagnetite never degrades.
book.magnetization.category.machines.des: Automation: generators, sensors, RNG, the tokamak, and the structural inducer.
book.magnetization.category.propulsion.d: Move your ships: repulsor tracks, Lenz braking, jets, thrusters, and sails.
book.magnetization.category.fluids.name: Fluids
book.magnetization.category.fluids.desc: Ferrofluid, magnetized ferrofluid, and magnetorheological fluid.
book.magnetization.category.gear.name: Gear & Utility
book.magnetization.category.gear.desc: Wearables, the ore compass, item frame, anvils, and diamagnetic tricks.
